title: pdftotext/pdfinfo subprocess: missing '--' separator enables flag-injection via hostile filename
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

**Dimension:** Security and Access / Functional Correctness

**Location:** /home/doll/OpenClaudia/src/tools/file/read.rs lines 116-117 (pdfinfo), 143-155 (pdftotext)

**Violation:**
read_pdf_file invokes 'pdfinfo <path>' and 'pdftotext [-f F -l L] <path> -' supplying the path as a separate arg. Although Command::arg() avoids shell-level injection, it does NOT protect against pdftotext's own option parsing. A file named '-help', '--version', '-opw', or '-upw' is interpreted as a flag by the subprocess. An attacker able to name a file in the project (via git clone of adversarial repo, extracted archive, /tmp traversal) can cause pdftotext to accept owner/user password args, hang waiting for stdin, print version, or skip output entirely.

Secondary issues:
1. pdfinfo output parsing is loose: 'line.starts_with("Pages:")' (line 121). A PDF whose metadata Title begins 'Pages:' or similar can spoof the count.
2. No bound on pdftotext stdout size — the truncation guard at read_text_file (line 320) is not applied here.

**Mandated Refactor:**
1. Insert '--' as the option terminator before the path arg in both pdfinfo and pdftotext invocations: cmd.arg("--").arg(path).
2. Canonicalize path first so it always begins with '/' which pdftotext never parses as a flag.
3. Anchor the pdfinfo regex: ^Pages:\s+(\d+)$.
4. Enforce max output size by capturing stdout into a capped buffer.
5. Reject page ranges that span >200 pages.
